perturbation.original_text: Upon the Disclosing Party’s request, the Receiving Party will promptly return to the Disclosing Party all materials or tangible items containing the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information and all copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Disclosing Party’s reasonable request, the Receiving Party will, within a reasonable timeframe, return the primary materials or tangible items containing the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information and endeavor to account for copies thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates a prompt return of all confidential materials and copies upon request. The modified text introduces ambiguity by adding 'reasonable request' and 'reasonable timeframe', making the obligation subjective. The term 'endeavor to account for copies thereof' weakens the requirement to return all copies.
perturbation.law_explanation: Virginia Code § 59.1-336 allows for injunctive relief to prevent misappropriation of trade secrets. The modification introduces doubt regarding the precise and timely return of all confidential materials, potentially hindering the Disclosing Party's ability to obtain an injunction if misappropriation is suspected because it's no longer clear what the exact obligation of return entails.

Once the report is generated you'll then have the option to download it as a pdf, print or email the report. Code of Virginia Title 59.1. Trade and Commerce Chapter 26. Uniform Trade Secrets Act 6/23/2025 § 59.1-336 . Short title and definitions. As used in this chapter, which may be cited as the Uniform Trade Secrets Act, unless the context requires otherwise: "Improper means" includes theft, bribery, misrepresentation, use of a computer or computer network without authority, breach of a duty or inducement of a breach of a duty to maintain secrecy, or espionage through electronic or other means. "Misappropriation" means: 1. Acquisition of a trade secret of another by a person who knows or has reason to know that the trade secret was acquired by improper means; or 2. Disclosure or use of a trade secret of another without express or implied consent by a person who a. Used improper means to acquire knowledge of the trade secret; or b. At the time of disclosure or use, knew or had reason to know that his knowledge of the trade secret was (1) Derived from or through a person who had utilized improper means to acquire it; (2) Acquired under circumstances giving rise to a duty to maintain its secrecy or limit its use; (3) Derived from or through a person who owed a duty to the person seeking relief to maintain its secrecy or limit its use; or (4) Acquired by accident or mistake. perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity regarding the return of confidential information, weakening the Disclosing Party's ability to enforce their rights and potentially hindering injunctive relief for misappropriation as described in the Law Explanation referencing Virginia Code § 59.1-336. The original contract mandated a prompt return of all materials and copies, whereas the changed text uses 'reasonable request' and 'reasonable timeframe,' making the obligation subjective. The addition of 'endeavor to account for copies thereof' further weakens the requirement to return all copies. Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines 'Misappropriation' as the acquisition of a trade secret by improper means or the disclosure/use of a trade secret without consent. The modified language creates a loophole, potentially allowing the Receiving Party to delay or avoid the return of confidential information, thereby increasing the risk of misappropriation.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall also have the right to pursue any other rights or remedies available at law or equity for such a breach.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party may explore the possibility of pursuing other potential rights or remedies, as allowed by law or equity, for a possible breach.
perturbation.explanation: The original text grants each party the right to pursue remedies. The modified text weakens this right by replacing 'shall have the right to pursue' with 'may explore the possibility of pursuing' and 'such a breach' with 'a possible breach', introducing uncertainty and making the enforcement of remedies less assured. It suggests an option rather than an entitlement.
perturbation.law_explanation: Virginia Code § 59.1-337 allows for damages for misappropriation. By weakening the assurance that a party can pursue legal remedies, the modified clause creates uncertainty about the availability of damages. The phrase 'may explore the possibility' contradicts the clear entitlement to damages provided under Virginia law.

Once the report is generated you'll then have the option to download it as a pdf, print or email the report. Code of Virginia Title 59.1. Trade and Commerce Chapter 26. Uniform Trade Secrets Act 6/23/2025 § 59.1-337 . Injunctive relief. A. Actual or threatened misappropriation may be enjoined. Upon application to the court, an injunction shall be terminated when the trade secret has ceased to exist, but the injunction may be continued for an additional reasonable period of time in order to eliminate commercial advantage that otherwise would be derived from the misappropriation. B. In exceptional circumstances, an injunction may condition future use upon payment of a reasonable royalty for no longer than the period of time for which use could have been prohibited. Exceptional circumstances include, but are not limited to, a material and prejudicial change of position prior to acquiring knowledge or reason to know of misappropriation that renders a prohibitive injunction inequitable. C. In appropriate circumstances, affirmative acts to protect a trade secret may be compelled by court order. 1986, c. 210. The chapters of the acts of assembly referenced in the historical citation at the end of this section may not constitute a comprehensive list of such chapters and may exclude chapters whose provisions have expired. perturbation.justification: The original contract grants each party the right to pursue remedies for a breach. The modified text weakens this right, replacing 'shall have the right to pursue' with 'may explore the possibility of pursuing' and 'such a breach' with 'a possible breach', introducing uncertainty and making the enforcement of remedies less assured. It suggests an option rather than an entitlement. According to the law explanation, Virginia Code § 59.1-337 allows for damages for misappropriation. By weakening the assurance that a party can pursue legal remedies, the modified clause creates uncertainty about the availability of damages, which may contradict the entitlement to damages provided under Virginia law. Snippet 1, § 59.1-337, discusses injunctive relief for misappropriation, implying a legal avenue for remedies, which the modified text potentially undermines. The change introduces ambiguity, affecting enforceability.
